title: Add autocue serve --reset-cache CLI flag
description: Operator-facing escape hatch: --reset-cache deletes <rekordbox_dir>/autocue_cache.sqlite (and WAL/SHM sidecars) before starting the server. Useful when debugging or after a schema bump.
acceptanceCriteria: autocue serve --reset-cache deletes the cache file (if any) before initializing CacheStore.
Missing cache file is not an error - flag is a no-op in that case.
WAL (.sqlite-wal) and SHM (.sqlite-shm) sidecar files are also removed.
tests/test_cli.py or similar covers the flag.
Help text describes the flag clearly.
Flag is documented in CLAUDE.md Development commands.

steps.details: autocue/cli.py: parser.add_argument('--reset-cache', action='store_true', help='Delete the sidecar analysis cache before starting (no effect if absent).'). Wire into the serve subcommand only - not the CLI cue generation path.

steps.description: Resolve cache path before CacheStore opens
steps.details: Before serve/deps.py:lifespan calls CacheStore.open_for(rekordbox_dir), if args.reset_cache: for suffix in ('', '-wal', '-shm'): path = os.path.join(rekordbox_dir, f'autocue_cache.sqlite{suffix}'); if os.path.exists(path): os.remove(path).
tests/test_cli.py: invoke autocue serve --reset-cache via a subprocess with a sandbox rekordbox_dir + pre-existing cache file. Assert file is gone before the server starts accepting connections.

technicalNotes: Don't rm -rf the whole rekordbox_dir - only the three cache files. Path traversal is impossible because the suffix is constant.
